items.title: From single perceptron to stacked layers
items.description: A perceptron is one output neuron (often with a step or sign activation). Layers & Neurons generalizes that unit into banks of parallel neurons organized into input, hidden, and output tiers.

items.title: Multilayer Perceptron as the full stack
items.description: Once you know layer roles, width, and depth, the Multilayer Perceptron topic wires them into a trainable model with backpropagation, loss, and optimization.

items.title: Matrix multiplication implements each layer
items.description: The forward pass through a dense layer is X @ W + b — batched matrix multiplication. Fluency with shapes and the row-by-column rule is required to read and debug layer code.

items.title: Linear combinations inside every neuron
items.description: Each neuron computes a weighted sum of inputs plus bias before activation. Forward Propagation — Linear Combinations explains that inner step that repeats at every unit in every layer.
